When a gas is exposed to a voltage greater than its insulating capabilities, electrical arcs result. The arc begins when the voltage is high enough to ionize the space between the conductors. A gas becomes a great conductor when it is ionized, allowing current to flow and producing the arc-shaped current known as an arc […]
